source_paper_title: Monotone Causality in Opportunistically Stochastic Shortest Path Problems
context:
Page 33, Section 6 (Conclusions)

"Another interesting direction for future work is to find criteria for applicability of label-setting to \“stochastic shortest path games,\” in which the probability distribution over the successor nodes at each stage depends on the actions chosen by two antagonistic players [46]. We note that some results on the applicability of Dijkstra’s method to deterministic games on graphs have been developed in the last ten years [7, 11], but the case of general stochastic games on graphs remains open."
title: Label-setting applicability to stochastic shortest path games (two-player stochastic games on graphs)
latex:
Given a finite directed graph with state set \(X = \{x_1,\dots,x_n,t\}\), where \(t\) is an absorbing terminal state, consider a two-player zero-sum stochastic shortest path game. At each nonterminal state \(x\in X\setminus\{t\}\), Player 1 chooses an action \(a\in A_1(x)\) and Player 2 chooses an action \(b\in A_2(x)\). The immediate cost incurred is \(C(x,a,b) > 0\), and the next state is drawn according to a known transition kernel \(p(\cdot\mid x,a,b)\) on \(X\) (with \(p(t\mid t,a,b)=1\) and \(C(t,a,b)=0\)).

For a (possibly history-dependent, randomized) pair of policies \((\pi_1,\pi_2)\), let \(J(x;\pi_1,\pi_2)\) be the expected total cost accumulated until reaching \(t\) when starting at \(x\). Define the game value function
\[
U(x) = \inf_{\pi_1}\sup_{\pi_2} J(x;\pi_1,\pi_2), \qquad x\in X.
\]
The corresponding dynamic programming (Shapley) optimality equations are
\[
U(t)=0,\qquad
U(x)=\min_{a\in A_1(x)}\max_{b\in A_2(x)}\Bigl(C(x,a,b)+\sum_{y\in X} p(y\mid x,a,b)U(y)\Bigr),\quad x\neq t.
\]

Problem: Find explicit, a priori verifiable conditions on the local data \((A_1(x),A_2(x),C(x,\cdot,\cdot),p(\cdot\mid x,\cdot,\cdot))\) under which a non-iterative label-setting algorithm (Dijkstra/Dial-type) is guaranteed to compute \(U\) correctly for such stochastic shortest path games (and, ideally, to do so in near-linear time in \(|X|\) under bounded outdegree).
open_problem_quote: "Another interesting direction for future work is to find criteria for applicability of label-setting to \“stochastic shortest path games,\” in which the probability distribution over the successor nodes at each stage depends on the actions chosen by two antagonistic players [46]. We note that some results on the applicability of Dijkstra’s method to deterministic games on graphs have been developed in the last ten years [7, 11], but the case of general stochastic games on graphs remains open."
subject_classification: Stochastic Games / Dynamic Programming / Shortest Path Problems
